text.title: Legal Compliance Checklist Stage-by-Stage Implementation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `ops-legal-compliance-checklist` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Progress only when the prior stage is fully checked off: Day-1 → Month-1 → Year-1 → Ongoing. Skipping stages or marking partial completion as done is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order completion creates compliance gaps that surface during audit. The stage gate forces sequential completion and a paper trail.

rule.statement: The checklist verifies presence and process, NOT legal sufficiency of text. Marking a ToS row 'done' because a file exists, without attorney review, is forbidden as evidence of legal sufficiency.
rule.rationale: Checklists confirm operational readiness; only an attorney confirms enforceability. Conflating the two creates false confidence.

rule.statement: Day-1 items (entity formation, EIN, business bank account, ToS / Privacy / Refund policies) MUST be checked off before accepting the first paying customer. Accepting customers without these is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Operating without Day-1 items exposes the operator to personal liability and regulator action. The Day-1 floor is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Every checked row carries a link to evidence (filed document, signed policy, screenshot of compliance UI). Unevidenced 'done' marks are flagged at review.
rule.rationale: Auditors require evidence per control. Pre-staging evidence at check-off time eliminates pre-audit panic.

rule.statement: Ongoing items (policy refresh cycle, vendor contract review, data-deletion drills) have named owners and review cadences (quarterly or annual). 'Whoever has time' is forbidden as cadence.
rule.rationale: Without named owners and cadence, ongoing items decay into stale folklore. The cadence + owner pair is the only reliable mechanism for keeping the program alive.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
